title: polyalthia biglandulosa
intro: Polyalthia biglandulosa, also known as the Bintangor tree or Bintangor tree, is a species of plant in the family Annonaceae, native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The bark of the Polyalthia biglandulosa plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, and is believed to possess medicinal properties such as analgesic, anti-arthritic, and antipyretic effects. The plant is also used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the bark of the Polyalthia biglandulosa plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tonics, and can also be used in the form of a paste or ointment to apply topically.
